hint.en: Inheritance, Polymorphism, Encapsulation, Abstraction
Ability to create new abstractions based on existing abstractions (classes) by deriving the existing behavior we can re-use, extend or modify the base class.
Is the property in a programming language that allows objects of different types to be substituted for one another in program flow without needing to know ahead of time what the object's type.
Protective encasement that permits access to an object's data only via specifically assigned operations.
Showing only the essential attributes of something and hiding any implementation information that is unnecessary to the user
A subclass extending a superclass

hint.es: Override y overloading son parte del polimorfismo, upcasting y downcasting también

hint.en: private fields, props (getter and setters), encapsulate what varies principle
Create private methods to hide implementation details, abstract out functions and methods that are common across multiple instances and create a single generalized concept

Abstract is used for the inherit chain, Interface defines a contract that must be implemented, abstract classes can have base implementation, interfaces usually only have props and methods definition (C# 8.0 allows interface default methods implementations)

**public**: type or member can be accessed from anywhere
**private**: type or member can be only accessed within the same class
**protected**: type or member can be accessed within the same class or a deriving class
**internal**: type or member can be accessed anywhere in the declaring assembly
**private protected**: can be accessed only in the declaring assembly by code in the same class or a derived one
**protected internal**: can be accessed only in declaring assembly, or by a deriving class in another assembly.

C# does not allow multiple inheritance through classes, we must implement multiple interfaces, see [deadly diamond of death problem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiple_inheritance#The_diamond_problem)
Declare a **virtual** method in a base class, then use **override** modifier in child class method, that way we modifiy the existing behavior (polymorphism)
Declare several methods with almost same signature except for parameters number or type

hint.en: Stack is a block of memory for storing local variables and parameters. Computer's Memory, Temprary Storage Memory

hint.en: These patterns are designed for class instantiation. They can be either class-creation patterns or object-creational patterns

These patterns are designed with regard to a class' structure and composition. The main goal of most of these patterns is to increase the functionality of the class(es) involved, without changin much of its composition
These patterns are designed depending on how one class communicates with others

hint.en: A Client is a piece of software that requests tokens from IdentityServer - either for authenticationg a user or for accessing a resource
APIs reousrces represent functionality a client want to invoke - typically modelled as Web APIs.
Identity information (aka claims) about a user, e.g. name of email address.
An identity token represents the outcome of an authentication process.
An access token allows access to an API Resource.
